metadata.title: The Little Girl & The Moon -- Accessible & Recreational Narratives
metadata.description:
This series of recreational materials was developed using resources provided by SEM Switzerland for asylum seekers, as well as some of its subcontractors such as ORS and Protectas.

It was created entirely using a basic Android phone, simple Android applications, and prompt-based interaction with widely available online AI tools, accessed via the public network.

The aim of these MP4 recreational materials, which were translated into Russian, English, French, Arabic, and German, is twofold.

 

First, to leverage the linguistic diversity present in the CFA centres.

Second, to complement existing video content that is broadcast mainly on visual terminals within these centres. 

 

At the end, we believe that, through simple drawing, writing, and the use of intelligent prompting, any asylum seeker could contribute to the creation of video or audio materials suitable for broadcast on these terminals.

To conclude, we summarise the reproducible steps as follows :

(1) We identified a visually appealing woodworking book by Bosch, in the swiss asylum center's library ;

(2) We took photographs of selected pages using a basic Android phone's camera ;

(3) We extracted images and ocerised text using native Android tools ;

(4) We used intelligent prompting to generate an allegorical and Phaedrus inspired recension of the wood assembly instructions, and translations ;

(5) We used a mainstream text-to-speech model to generate the mp3 (from ~9000 characters) ;

(6) We sketched drawn recensions of the wood assembly photographs, and took pictures to create a further diaporama ;

(7) We use the phone's native screen recorder while manually playing the mp3s and "playing" with the diaporama ;

(8) We use a mainstream phone-based video editor to reduce resolution and I terchange audio parts (whence targeted languages)

Please note that the redrawing step was not strictly necessary; however, it offers two advantages: it allows contributors to express their artistic creativity, and it helps avoid the misuse of copyrighted material by recreating the content in an original creative recension.

All the best.

data.title: The Little Girl & The Moon -- Accessible & Recreational Narratives

metadata.title: ADAPTATION OF THE CAREER ADAPTABILITY SCALE (CAAS) MEASUREMENT TOOL FOR GENERATION Z JOB SEEKER IN YOGYAKARTA
metadata.description: Study this aim for evaluate the adaptation and validation process tool measure the Indonesian version of the Career Adapt-Abilities Scale (CAAS) in context seeker Work Generation Z in Yogyakarta. Approach study use method quantitative with purposive sampling technique, involving 177 searchers Work Generation Z. Procedure adaptation follow Beaton et al.'s (2000) guidelines, which include forward translation, back translation, synthesis results translation, as well as expert review. Validity test construct done using Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA). The results show that model four CAAS dimensions, namely concern, control, curiosity, and confidence, meet goodness of fit criteria (CFI = 0.922; TLI = 0.912; RMSEA = 0.0733; SRMR = 0.0451). Total reliability of the scale reached 0.785, and each subscale show adequate internal consistency. Findings This indicates that the Indonesian version of CAAS is valid and reliable for used in context Indonesian culture, especially for those seeking Work Generation Z in Yogyakarta. Instruments This can used for measure adaptability career , as well as become base for study advanced related development career generation young people in Indonesia.
keywords: Adaptability Career Adapt Abilities Scale, Validity , Reliability , Search Work Generation Z

metadata.title: Zuhud in the Perspective of Sufism: Its Concept, Implementation, And Relevance in Modern Life
metadata.description: Zuhud is one of the fundamental concepts in Sufism that emphasizes moderation in worldly affairs and wholehearted devotion to Allah SWT. In the context of modern society, characterized by materialism, hedonism, and increasing psychological distress, the values embodied in zuhud offer a meaningful spiritual response. This study aims to examine the concept of zuhud from the perspective of Sufism, explore its implementation in the life of a sālik (spiritual seeker), and analyze its relevance to contemporary life. The study employed a qualitative approach using a library research design by analyzing classical Sufi texts and relevant contemporary literature. Data were collected through documentation techniques and analyzed using descriptive-analytical methods. The findings reveal that zuhud does not imply complete withdrawal from worldly life; rather, it involves placing worldly possessions and interests in their proper proportion while maintaining spiritual closeness to Allah SWT. Furthermore, the principles of zuhud remain highly relevant in modern life, as they encourage simplicity, self-restraint, inner peace, and spiritual awareness amid the pressures of contemporary society. Therefore, zuhud can be understood not only as an ascetic practice within Sufism but also as an ethical and spiritual framework for addressing the moral and existential challenges of the modern era.

metadata.title: THE INTERPRETATION OF DIVINE LOVE IN ROQIM'S POETRY
metadata.description: Annotation: This article examines the interpretation of divine love in the poetry of Roqim, one of the prominent representatives of classical Uzbek literature. It demonstrates that, in Roqim’s poetry, love is not merely an earthly emotion but a sacred force leading the seeker toward spiritual enlightenment and union with the Truth. Through textual analysis, the research highlights the poet’s contribution to the development of the tradition of divine-love poetry in Uzbek classical literature and reveals the humanistic and moral values embedded in his lyrical heritage.
references: 1.Ўзбек адабиёти тарихи. Беш томлик, 3 том. Тошкент, 1977.

metadata.title: Colonization and the Form of Violance in Coetzee's Novel Dusklands
metadata.description: Implicit in Coetzee’s rejection of the realist model is the desire to re-present the acts of violence which constitute a recurring subject in his fiction, without participating in the process of violation which those acts exhibit. Coetzee’s renunciation of the realist model also enables him to engage in a second project, one closely associated with the attempt to depict violence without inviting sensationalism. This paper may be as an attempt to counter history as a prison, one in which we are doomed to repeat without difference the contours of a violent past. This past, Coetzee argues, has ensured a present which remains essentially “colonial and is characterised by its inability to sustain itself except through repeated acts of violation”. (“Colonialism” 370) Coetzee’s first novel, Dusklands (1974), demonstrates both thematically and formally these two characteristics of Coetzee’s fiction — its awareness of the propensity for a fictional ‘treatment’ of violence to subvert itself into the form of a fantasy which is pornographic in its effects, and its concern to deconstruct the repetitive structures of colonialism.

metadata.title: 量子结构性失业:经典结构性失业理论的量子重构与一般化拓展(Quantum Structural Unemployment:Quantum Reconstruction and Generalized Expansion of Classical Structural Unemployment Theory)
metadata.description:
摘 要:经典结构性失业理论基于机械论世界观,将失业归因于技能错配、产业结构转型和工资刚性等确定性因素,认为消除错配与摩擦可实现市场出清。此框架是劳动经济学理论基础,但与现实劳动力市场有系统性偏离:“有岗无人、有人无岗”现象普遍且长期存在,求职者和企业多观望犹豫,就业结果依赖预期与信心;相同技能与岗位条件在不同预期下匹配效率不同,就业状态呈跃迁式变化。传统理论把这些现象归为摩擦性失业或制度性约束,未从本体论层面揭示结构性失业的量子本质。本文在纯经济学范式下构建量子结构性失业(Quantum Structural Unemployment,QSU)理论,提出劳动市场状态叠加、技能—预期相干、求职者—岗位纠缠、观测—坍缩与劳动力市场熵五大核心公理,给出量子失业率的统一形式化表达。本文严格证明:经典结构性失业、摩擦性失业、周期性失业与工资刚性失业均是量子结构性失业在特定约束条件下的退化特例。量子结构性失业理论突破了“技能—岗位一一对应、市场必然出清”的机械思维,统一解释了匹配非出清、就业观望、预期驱动失业与状态跃迁等全场景劳动力市场现象,为就业政策制定、劳动力市场治理与宏观调控提供了更具现实解释力的统一基础范式。

关键词:量子结构性失业;技能错配;预期相干;求职者—岗位纠缠;观测—坍缩

data.title: 量子结构性失业:经典结构性失业理论的量子重构与一般化拓展(Quantum Structural Unemployment:Quantum Reconstruction and Generalized Expansion of Classical Structural Unemployment Theory)
